And also Josh here, one of the implementation specialists, and I want to go ahead and walk you through exactly how do you use this Leads and Stats Sheet? Okay, it's gonna be one of the most important sheets that you have because this is where everything connects together and also gives you your stats. Okay, so what will happen here is you'll see three tabs on the bottom here, let me move myself out of the way. You'll see three tabs on the bottom, you're tracking your stats and your incoming leads. So, kind of giving an overview is in here in your first columns all the way up until each, these things are all locked on the tracking sheet. So if you actually, let's just say we had test here and her last name was incorrect and I want to edit her last name, right? It's actually not going to let me do it and it's gonna change it back to where it needs to be. Or if you do go ahead and click through and you hit okay that you still wanna change it, you'll see it's gonna break that entire row. So, what does that mean? It means that we don't want to change columns A through H on the tracking sheet, okay? Now what you can edit is you can edit anything after H here. So if you want to add in additional columns here, insert one right, you can go ahead and actually do that and then name it whatever you want it to be. And that will be for your tracking purposes on your lead nurture or just notes on the prospect themselves, okay? Then you have your incoming lead sheet and this is where you would go if you wanted to actually edit any contact information. So let's just say maybe the appointment time did not pull from acuity and you wanna actually go in here and you wanna edit that or update it for some reason, right? Or maybe their email needs to be updated or phone number was not correct or maybe you even just wanna come to the bottom and add in a contact who maybe did it come from your click funnels or acuity, okay? Then this is where you would go to do that. So let's talk about how the stats will pull from your tracking sheet. So the first thing I want you to notice is that on this sheet, there are two things that are highlighted, your ad spend and your unique clicks. And so it is up to you to update these. I recommend for your first time running this program that you're updating this on a daily basis just to get in the habit of it every single morning you should be checking your ads anyways. So this is where you would update your total ad spend and your total unique clicks. So you'll get that in your ads manager here. You go to the campaign that you're running, make sure you're on the campaign level and make sure that you're showing, if you're running multiple campaigns, then make sure you have all of them here because you're gonna total up your amount spent, okay? And so if you scroll over in our columns for reporting, you'll see where it says amount spent. This one is automatically on here on your columns. So you'll be able to see the total. So for this campaign, I spent 413 bucks. And then for this campaign, I spent 161. So if you were running two campaigns, you would just add them together, only add in the ones that are active campaigns. If you're only running one campaign, then let's just say I'm only running one right now and this is free six weeks. And I would add in the 413.75. I would take that number and put that over here on my ad spend, okay? So now you want to figure out the unique clicks. So how do you find the unique clicks? For most people, the unique clicks will actually not be on this column. So you have to actually hit this drop down here, which is columns custom, click that. And then you can customize your columns. You'll click on that, it will open up a screen. And on this screen here, you're gonna just type in the search bar unique and you'll see where it says unique clicks. Mine was already checked because I had it on there, but you'll want to go ahead and check that box. Come to the bottom here, hit apply. And then now if you scroll all the way over, you'll see that unique clicks count and then that will go in there as well, okay? And I just want to explain what this is telling us. So the reason why we want the ad spend is because now that we have the ad spend, we have the total leads, now this sheet will automatically tell you what is your cost per lead, okay? And the reason why you want to know the unique clicks is because the unique clicks is gonna tell you how many people went to your landing page, which will give us our funnel conversion number here, okay? And so a good example of why this matters is because if your funnel is converting under 10%, that's probably gonna be why you have a high cost per lead, which means that it's not necessary that you need to actually change your ads and run new Facebook ads, and maybe that you need to change your landing page because you're sending traffic, you're actually getting a lot of people to click on the ads, but when they go to the landing page, they're not actually opting in and becoming a lead, okay? So that's why it's important to understand those stats so that way you can make the right decisions regarding your marketing, okay? Now let's talk about everything that happened after the marketing, such as your scheduled, your showed, your closed, and the percentage of leads that you are selling, okay? So in a video further down in your overview section, Alice is gonna talk about stats and what those stats mean and what you should be aiming for. So I'm not gonna go too much in detail, but I do wanna show you what stats you're pulling from this sheet, okay? So the first two stats is you know your cost per lead, you know your funnel conversion. Now we wanna know what percentage of your leads actually schedule appointments, right? And so what this would show us is how many of your leads are not scheduled and how many of our leads are scheduled, which shows me that right now we have a 45% schedule rate on R of leads, right? And so you want to be somewhere above 50, 55%, 60% is an average number, okay? It will also show you how many of those appointments are upcoming, it will show you how many of the scheduled appointments that you had actually showed up for their appointments, and then it will actually show you how many canceled and then how many no showed, all right? Which will give us our show percentage, okay? And you want to definitely be over 30% when it comes to your show rate. Then we're down here, we'll have the number of sold, which will show us the percentage of people that we sold that showed up. So this is our closing percentage, and then you have the percentage of leads will mean how many, what percent of your total leads, right? So what percent of all the leads that we have actually signed up for the program? The amount here is going to be the total packages amount. So for example, if you sold, you know, 10 people at 699, and that would say 6,999, okay? So then you have your return, and your return is going to be your return on investment. So what this means is the return on your ad spend, right? So, you know, for me, I spent 413 bucks, and I made $2,396, which means that I have a 579% return, a 5x return, right? Which is good, very good, right? And so the reason why that is important is because you want to pay attention to that because if you have a 579% return, even if your stats aren't great here, where should you put your energy and effort at, right? It would be in let's just keep getting that return. So how could we keep getting that return? We just keep spending money on Facebook, okay? So that's where you want to see the bigger picture and know that the number that matters the most is what is your return on your investment, okay? Because if it's positive, that means you can continue to acquire clients for free, all right? Actually getting paid to acquire clients, all right? So that's a big one to know. Now, Sabrina in the last video probably already showed you how to do this, but just make sure to get the keep fee stats for these stats that actually mean anything to you, that means that this needs to be updated always. So what I recommend is the end of every single sales day or just every single day at the end, you come through and you mark the people who were scheduled, did they show? Yes, did they cancel? If they cancel, that just means they mentioned above beforehand that they were not coming in or you canceled their appointment, or if they were in no show, which means that they had no communication to you and they just did not show up, right? You'll check the box if they were sold, leave it unchecked if they were not. The total package amount is going to be how much did this program cost, right? So if you're selling it for 599, then you should have 599 in every single column, okay? The total upfront cash is going to mean or it might say deposit in your sheet. And what that just means is how much did you actually collect the same day? Because in a perfect world, you would always collect the 599 upfront, but sometimes we will have to put down half or put down 100 bucks or whatever you take if you do payment plans, and that would go right here. Reason you want to know that number is because your upfront cash is how much you actually pocket it, and you want to know that because that number should always be more than your Facebook ad spend, which means that you can continue to spend on Facebook ads. Your notes here is going to be up to you what you want to put. This might be notes about the sales appointment, or this might be your lead nurture notes that you put in after you're actually reaching out to your leads, okay? So guys, that is how you use your leads in stat sheets. Super important sheet, make sure that you're keeping it updated, and you guys enjoy.
